evidence.quote: All cells release exosomes, 30-150 nm membrane-bound vesicles
Most cell types in the central nervous system (CNS) release exosomes, including astrocytes, microglia, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
astrocyte-derived extracellular vesicles (ADEVs) were isolated from human primary astrocytes in culture
evaluated using mass spectrometry

proteomic signature of exosomes derived from astrocytes revealed astrocyte-specific markers including GFAP, excitatory amino acid transporter 1 (SLC1A3/GLAST), and glucose transporter member 1 (SLCA1/GLUT1)
Proteomic profiling demonstrated that a fraction of these exosomes contained canonical myelin proteins, including myelin proteolipid protein (PLP)...
Dynamic light scattering (DLS) detected three distinct populations of EVs
finding that the proportion of exosomes around 100 nm in diameter was significantly increased in the GD cohort
Complementary studies using cryo-EM showed an abundance of vesicles with altered morphology
High resolution images of EVs isolated from these patients exhibited multilayered vesicles of larger size, when compared to controls
evaluated by assays measuring the cleavage product of the soluble lysosomal enzyme N-acetyl-β-D-glucosaminidase (NAG)
amount of NAG released from cultured control fibroblast cell lines was 250-fold higher than the amount released in lines from patients with MLIV
Lipid extracts from exosomes isolated from primary cultured oligodendrocytes were analyzed by thin layer chromatography (TLC)
These studies identified an enrichment of the canonical myelin lipids galactocerebroside and sulfatide
producing enzyme-loaded HEK293-derived exosomes for the targeted delivery of glucocerebrosidase
The exosomes were taken up by recipient cells, which showed significantly enhanced glucocerebrosidase activity
loaded naïve EVs with TPP1 therapeutic enzyme ex vitro, using sonication or saponin permeabilization
Around 70% of the enzyme was delivered to lysosomes, the target organelles
Using a late-infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis mouse model
noted an increased lifespan after intraperitoneal administration of EV-TPP1
when wild-type N-acetyl-α-neuraminidase activity was restored in the neu1-/- murine myofibroblasts
exosomes isolated from the rescued cell line no longer converted normal fibroblasts into myofibroblasts

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: Depletion of MLN1 from mature adipocytes reduced the exosomal markers CD9, CD81, CD63, and Hsp70
loss of MLN1 led to a 50% reduction in levels of the lysosomal marker LAMP-1

issues.reason: The specific quantitative observation (50% reduction, CD9/CD81 etc.) is explicitly linked to MLN1 (MLIV) and adipocytes in the text. Sialidosis is mentioned regarding LAMP-1 redistribution but not these specific marker reductions or percentages.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Restrict parameters to MLIV and mature adipocytes for the specific 50% LAMP-1 reduction and CD9/CD81 marker claim, or separate the Sialidosis LAMP-1 redistribution finding.

evidence.quote: exosomes secreted by cortical neurons upon glutamatergic synapse activation were selectively endocytosed by neurons
exosomes derived from neuroblastoma cells were taken up by glial and neuronal cells without bias
